summary: Tested Document Upload/Download feature (Emergent object storage). Backend 100% passing (11/11 pytest). Frontend partially working: upload, download (blob), category filter, and modal UI all work; a real-time list refresh issue was observed after in-modal upload (and possibly after delete) where the new card does not appear/old card does not disappear without a full session restart, even though the backend API has the correct soft-delete/insert applied.

ui_bugs.issue: After successful upload via the modal, toast 'Documento caricato' fires and modal closes, but the new document card does NOT appear in the grid even after waiting 4s. A fresh page navigation (logout/login or hard reload via Documents menu re-click) shows the doc correctly. Same symptom seen after delete: the deleted card remains visible despite backend confirming soft-delete. Network tab confirms /api/documents IS re-fetched and returns 200 after the mutation.
Console warning: Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby={undefined}` for {DialogContent}. Add a <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby to satisfy Radix accessibility.

integration_issues.issue: POST /api/documents/upload returns 200 with the new doc; subsequent GET /api/documents (triggered by load()) also returns 200 and contains the new doc in DB, yet React state/grid doesn't reflect it within the same session. Suspected stale closure or unmount race when Dialog closes.
DELETE /api/documents/{id} executes (DB has is_deleted=true) but the card stays in the rendered grid until manual reload.

action_items: Investigate Documents.jsx load() refresh after onDone(): suggest awaiting load() before setOpen(false), e.g. `onDone={async () => { setOpen(false); await load(); }}`. Also consider passing a fresh callback via useCallback or storing docs via React Query to ensure no stale closure.
Add a DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to the Nuovo documento DialogContent to remove Radix accessibility warning.
Optional: have the upload endpoint response push into local docs state directly (setDocs(prev => [doc, ...prev])) instead of relying on a refetch.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 60-66: when storage init fails, the function returns None silently; init_storage() is then re-tried on every put/get. OK but logs only WARN at startup. Consider failing the upload endpoint earlier with a clear 503.
Documents.jsx: load() is recreated on every render and not memoized. UploadForm's onDone closes over a possibly stale `load` reference relative to the open modal lifecycle. Use useCallback or a ref.
Backend GET /api/documents/{id}/download: filename in Content-Disposition is not RFC 5987 escaped (would break for accented filenames like 'Contratto è.pdf'). Use filename* / urllib.parse.quote.
Documents.jsx download(): when storage_path is missing the legacy doc path opens doc.url with window.open — but legacy seeded docs have no url either, only a toast. Acceptable but worth noting in UX.

success_rate.frontend: 75% (upload network OK, download OK, filters OK, modal OK; list refresh fails)

seed_data_creation: All test docs were prefixed TEST_ and cleaned up via the API at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_documents_upload.py and pass 100%. Use BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://visit-planner-32.preview.emergentagent.com. The known frontend bug is that after upload (and after delete) within the same session, the docs grid in /documenti does not refresh to reflect the change even though /api/documents is re-fetched and returns the new state. Fix candidate: in Documents.jsx pass `onDone={async () => { setOpen(false); await load(); }}` or refactor to manage docs via state-update-on-response.
rca_of_the_issue: Backend storage_put/get works correctly with Emergent objstore (verified via download bytes equality). The 'Object storage initialized' log appears once at startup as expected. Frontend list refresh failure: hypothesis is that UploadForm calls onDone() which schedules both setOpen(false) and load() (non-awaited). When setOpen(false) unmounts the Dialog body the parent re-renders, possibly recreating closures. Combined with React 18 batching, the setDocs(d.data) update may be applied to a state slice that is subsequently overwritten/ignored. Reproduction: login → /documenti → click 'Carica documento' → pick PDF → save → toast appears, modal closes, but new card does not show until user navigates away and back (or full reload triggers fresh useEffect).
